context.project_description: A system that tracks LLM token usage per query to provide observability into cost consumption across different LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama), integrating with existing benchmarking instrumentation framework for both query and chat endpoints.
key_requirements: Instrument chat methods (chat, chat_stream) in all current LLM providers to collect token counts
Integrate with the existing InstrumentedProxy infrastructure used for benchmarking
Support both synchronous and streaming calls across all provider types
constraints: Must work within existing abstraction layer via ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider protocols in base.py
Must be compatible with InstrumentedProxy infrastructure used for benchmarking
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py, fitz_ai/logging/logger.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
stakeholders: SDK Users who want to know how much their queries cost without modifying code
Platform Engineers who need reliable metric reporting and minimal perf impact
Product Managers who are interested in cost transparency for AI features

in_scope: Instrument chat methods (chat, chat_stream) in all current LLM providers
Collect and expose token counts from each call via logging or structured metrics
Integrate with the benchmark hooks system to avoid duplicating logic
Support both synchronous and streaming calls
out_of_scope: Dashboards or cost projections based on model prices

existing_files: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients.
fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance.
fitz_ai/logging/logger.py — Unified logging setup for the entire Fitz project.
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_tracker.py — Contains logic for wrapping chat methods and collecting token counts using InstrumentedProxy.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai_with_tracking.py — Wrapper module around existing OpenAI provider to inject tracking logic.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic_with_tracking.py — Wrapper module around existing Anthropic provider to inject tracking logic.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere_with_tracking.py — Wrapper module around existing Cohere provider to inject tracking logic.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama_with_tracking.py — Wrapper module around existing Ollama provider to inject tracking logic.

assumptions.assumption: Each provider returns a token count or has access to one during call (e.g., from OpenAI SDK)
assumptions.impact: Need manual parsing or custom logic if no built-in support exists

assumptions.assumption: Token counts are returned directly by providers, e.g., response metadata
assumptions.impact: Must implement custom counting fallbacks if not exposed

assumptions.assumption: Tokens should be logged in each query context with structured logger (from `logger.py`)
assumptions.impact: May require metric collection layer addition later

assumptions.assumption: Each token count per call will be tracked independently unless specified otherwise
assumptions.impact: Future expansion may require aggregation logic

assumptions.assumption: Streaming responses are returned as generators and can be instrumented by wrapping `chat_stream` method
assumptions.impact: Need careful handling of generator state if logging inside iteration

approaches.name: Direct Hook Integration with InstrumentedProxy (Recommended)
approaches.description: Leverages existing `InstrumentedProxy` infrastructure from `fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py` to wrap chat methods (`chat`, `chat_stream`) in all LLM provider classes. Each wrapped method logs token usage via benchmark hooks, using the same mechanism that powers performance benchmarking today.
pros: Integrates seamlessly with current observability stack (`BenchmarkHook` system)
Reuses existing patterns (wrapping, hooking) from benchmarking
Maintains backward compatibility by wrapping rather than modifying base protocols
Minimal operational burden - relies entirely on existing infrastructure
cons: Requires modification to `InstrumentedProxy` internals to capture raw SDK responses for token extraction
Provider-specific token extraction logic must be implemented per SDK due to varying APIs
Streaming chat provider token aggregation not supported without changes to current proxy layer
Failure mode with missing token data requires custom error handling
best_for: Integrating token tracking into existing LLM provider infrastructure
Maintaining alignment with current benchmarking patterns

approaches.name: Centralized TokenTracker Service
approaches.description: Introduces a new service layer specifically for token tracking that intercepts all LLM calls and aggregates usage metrics.
pros: Single source of truth for token data across all providers
Can provide cost calculations if extended with pricing models
cons: Requires introducing a new service dependency outside current architectural patterns
Risks breaking existing code paths due to additional abstraction layer
More complex implementation than leveraging existing infrastructure
best_for: Scenarios requiring centralized token management and cost calculations

architecture.reasoning: This approach is right because it leverages the existing instrumentation infrastructure that's already used for benchmarking, ensuring consistency and minimal operational burden. It reuses proven patterns from the codebase without introducing new dependencies. The other approach (Centralized TokenTracker Service) is wrong because it introduces an unnecessary service layer that would break existing architectural patterns and require significant changes to integrate properly with the current LLM provider system.

key_tradeoffs.InstrumentedProxy Integration vs New Ser: Using existing InstrumentedProxy avoids introducing new service dependencies but requires modifying core instrumentation logic to capture SDK responses for token extraction
key_tradeoffs.Provider-Specific Logic vs Generic Appro: Implementing provider-specific token extraction handles API variations but increases complexity and maintenance burden compared to a generic approach

technology_considerations: InstrumentedProxy must be modified to store raw SDK responses during method execution so hooks can access them for token extraction
OpenAI requires extracting tokens from response.usage.prompt_tokens and response.usage.completion_tokens
Anthropic requires extracting tokens from response.usage.input_tokens and response.usage.output_tokens
Cohere requires parsing nested structure response.meta.billed_units.input_tokens
Ollama may not expose token usage in standard SDK responses requiring either workaround or TokenTrackingError handling
BenchmarkHook system needs access to original SDK response objects during on_call_end for token extraction
architecture.scope_statement: This task produces five new Python files (one core module + four provider-specific wrappers) that integrate token tracking into existing LLM providers via the `InstrumentedProxy` infrastructure, requiring modifications to InstrumentedProxy internals to capture SDK responses for token extraction.

adrs.title: ADR: Direct Hook Integration with InstrumentedProxy for Token Tracking
adrs.context: Need to instrument LLM providers to track token usage for observability and cost monitoring without introducing new service dependencies or breaking existing patterns.
adrs.decision: Leverage existing InstrumentedProxy infrastructure from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py to wrap chat methods in all LLM provider classes, using the same mechanism that powers performance benchmarking today.
adrs.rationale: This approach integrates seamlessly with current observability stack (BenchmarkHook system), reuses established patterns from benchmarking codebase, maintains backward compatibility by wrapping rather than modifying base protocols, and requires minimal operational burden since it relies entirely on existing infrastructure.
consequences: Minimal operational overhead as it leverages existing instrumentation framework
Requires provider-specific token extraction logic due to varying SDK APIs for accessing usage data
May fail loudly when token metadata is missing from responses (e.g., Ollama) rather than silently masking issues
Enables tracking of raw token counts only, avoiding complexity of cost calculations or currency conversions
alternatives_considered: Centralized TokenTracker Service - rejected because it would introduce new service layer not aligned with current architectural patterns and risks breaking existing code paths

Decorator Factory Pattern - rejected due to increased complexity around meta-programming, potential conflicts with module loading/testing, and unnecessary abstraction over existing InstrumentedProxy + hook system

adrs.title: ADR: Provider-Specific Token Extraction Logic Implementation
adrs.context: Each LLM SDK (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) has different response structures for accessing token usage information.
adrs.decision: Implement provider-specific extraction functions within each wrapper module rather than trying to create generic extractors that handle all variations.
adrs.rationale: This approach ensures accurate and reliable token data collection while acknowledging the fundamental differences in how each SDK exposes usage metrics. It allows for proper error handling when specific providers don't expose token information at all (like some Ollama versions).
consequences: Each provider module requires custom logic to parse response structures appropriately
Error handling must be robust across different SDK versions and behaviors
Adds complexity in maintaining separate extraction paths but ensures correctness
Provides clear documentation of which providers support token tracking vs those that don't
alternatives_considered: Generic TokenExtractor Protocol - rejected because the variations in response structure make this impractical; would require extensive conditional logic or additional abstraction layers
Shared Base Extractor with Plugin Architecture - rejected as overkill for this use case and adds unnecessary complexity to an already complex integration point

adrs.title: ADR: Streaming Chat Provider Token Aggregation Limitations
adrs.context: The current InstrumentedProxy + BenchmarkHook infrastructure cannot reliably aggregate token usage from streaming responses in real-time.
adrs.decision: Track total tokens consumed per chunk streamed and log aggregate totals after the stream ends, rather than attempting to provide intermediate breakdowns during streaming.
adrs.rationale: This approach maintains observability capabilities without requiring invasive changes to how streaming is handled. It's acceptable for current visibility needs while avoiding complexity in implementing real-time token aggregation that may not be critical for most use cases.
consequences: Loss of granular timing breakdowns during streaming operations
Only aggregate token counts available post-stream completion
May miss intermediate telemetry points important for certain monitoring scenarios
Simplifies implementation while still providing meaningful cost visibility
alternatives_considered: Streaming Hook Integration - rejected because the InstrumentedProxy model doesn't support real-time access to streaming chunks in hooks, requiring significant architectural changes
Custom Streaming Wrapper Layer - rejected due to high complexity and potential for introducing bugs or inconsistencies with existing streaming behavior

adrs.title: ADR: Feature Scope Limitations for v1 Implementation
adrs.context: Initial token tracking implementation should be focused and avoid scope creep into more complex features.
adrs.decision: Limit token tracking to raw count collection only, excluding cost calculation or aggregation by model type in the initial version.
adrs.rationale: This simplifies both implementation and maintenance while providing the core observability benefit needed. Cost calculations introduce currency exchange complications and vary across providers, making them better suited for future enhancements rather than immediate requirements.
consequences: Only raw token counts available without monetary cost estimates
Avoids complexity of handling different currencies or pricing models per provider
Simplifies testing and reduces risk of errors in billing calculations
Maintains focus on core observability functionality
alternatives_considered: Full Cost Tracking System - rejected because it would add significant complexity including currency conversion, provider-specific pricing tiers, and maintenance overhead that isn't needed for initial implementation
Model-Aware Aggregation Layer - rejected due to increased architectural complexity without immediate value proposition

adrs.title: ADR: Instrumentation Hook Modification Requirements
adrs.context: Current InstrumentedProxy does not expose raw SDK responses to hooks, which is required for token extraction from provider responses.
adrs.decision: Modify InstrumentedProxy internals to capture and store raw SDK response objects so that BenchmarkHooks can access them during on_call_end.
adrs.rationale: This change enables the hook-based approach to work correctly by providing access to the original SDK responses needed to extract token information, which is a fundamental requirement for this implementation to function properly.
consequences: Requires modification of core InstrumentedProxy functionality in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
May introduce subtle behavioral changes or performance implications if not implemented carefully
Creates dependency on updated instrumentation layer across entire codebase that uses benchmark hooks
Necessary prerequisite before token tracking system can function reliably
alternatives_considered: Workaround with Thread-Local Storage - rejected because it's fragile, harder to debug, and doesn't integrate well with existing hook patterns
Refactor All Hooks to Access Context Differently - rejected as excessive refactoring that breaks backward compatibility for other uses of the instrumentation system

components.name: TokenTrackerModule
components.purpose: Exposes a factory function to wrap LLM providers with token tracking capabilities using InstrumentedProxy infrastructure.
responsibilities: Wrap chat methods (chat, chat_stream) in LLM provider classes with token tracking
Integrate with existing BenchmarkHook system for logging token usage
Handle provider-specific token extraction logic
interfaces: wrap_with_token_tracking(provider: ChatProvider | StreamingChatProvider, methods_to_track: List[str]) -> InstrumentedProxy

responsibilities: Wrap OpenAIChat with token tracking capabilities
Extract token counts from OpenAI SDK responses
Maintain identical behavior to original OpenAIChat

components.name: OllamaWithTracking
components.purpose: Provide a wrapped version of Ollama chat provider that tracks token usage.
responsibilities: Wrap OllamaChat with token tracking capabilities
Handle cases where Ollama may not expose token metadata
Maintain identical behavior to original OllamaChat

artifacts.content:
from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, List, Optional, Tuple
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import InstrumentedProxy, maybe_wrap, BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider


@dataclass
class TokenUsageLog:
    model: str
    input_tokens: Optional[int] = None
    output_tokens: Optional[int] = None
    total_tokens: Optional[int] = None

class TokenTrackingError(Exception):
    """Raised when token usage cannot be extracted from LLM response."""
    pass


# Provider-specific extraction functions must exist in this file or imported modules

def _extract_token_usage_from_response(response, model_name: str) -> TokenUsageLog:
    # Placeholder - actual implementation varies by provider
    raise NotImplementedError("Must implement per-provider")


class TokenTrackerHook(BenchmarkHook):
    def on_call_start(self, method_name: str, args: Tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any:
        pass

    def on_call_end(self, method_name: str, result: Any, duration: float) -> None:
        # Must retrieve original SDK response to extract usage
        # Current InstrumentedProxy does not capture or expose this by default
        # This implementation is incomplete without changes to InstrumentedProxy internals
        pass


def wrap_with_token_tracking(provider: ChatProvider | StreamingChatProvider,
                            methods_to_track: List[str] = ['chat', 'chat_stream']) \
    -> InstrumentedProxy:

    wrapped = maybe_wrap(
        target=provider,
        layer='token_tracked',
        plugin_name='token_tracker',
        methods_to_track=methods_to_track
    )
    
    # Register hook for token collection after wrapping
    from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import register_hook
    
    tracker_hook = TokenTrackerHook()
    register_hook(tracker_hook)
    
    return wrapped
artifacts.purpose: Core module that provides the token tracking infrastructure including data models, error handling, and the main wrapping function that integrates with InstrumentedProxy.

artifacts.content:
import openai
from typing import Iterator, List, Optional, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass

from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai import OpenAIChat, OpenAIEmbedding, OpenAIVision
from fitz_ai.llm.instrumentation.token_tracker import wrap_with_token_tracking


# Wrap base OpenAI chat provider with token tracking
openai_chat = OpenAIChat()
wrapped_openai_chat = wrap_with_token_tracking(openai_chat)
artifacts.purpose: Provider-specific wrapper module for OpenAI that wraps the base OpenAIChat provider with token tracking functionality.

import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import InstrumentedProxy, maybe_wrap, BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider


@dataclass
class TokenUsageLog:
    model: str

    output_tokens: Optional[int] = None
    total_tokens: Optional[int] = None

    """Raised when token usage cannot be extracted from LLM response."""
    pass

def _extract_token_usage_from_response(response, model_name: str) -> TokenUsageLog:
    # Placeholder - actual implementation varies by provider
    raise NotImplementedError("Must implement per-provider")


class TokenTrackerHook(BenchmarkHook):
    def on_call_start(self, method_name: str, args: Tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any:
        pass

    def on_call_end(self, method_name: str, result: Any, duration: float) -> None:
        # Must retrieve original SDK response to extract usage
        # Current InstrumentedProxy does not capture or expose this by default
        # This implementation is incomplete without changes to InstrumentedProxy internals
        pass


def wrap_with_token_tracking(provider: ChatProvider | StreamingChatProvider,
                            methods_to_track: List[str] = ['chat', 'chat_stream']) \
    -> InstrumentedProxy:

    wrapped = maybe_wrap(
        target=provider,

from typing import Iterator, List, Optional, Any
import cohere

from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.cohere import CohereChat, CohereEmbedding, CohereRerank
from fitz_ai.llm.instrumentation.token_tracker import wrap_with_token_tracking


# Wrap base Cohere chat provider with token tracking
cohere_chat = CohereChat()
wrapped_cohere_chat = wrap_with_token_tracking(cohere_chat)
artifacts.purpose: Provider-specific wrapper module for Cohere that wraps the base CohereChat provider with token tracking functionality.

artifacts.content:
from typing import Iterator, List, Optional, Any

from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.ollama import OllamaChat, OllamaEmbedding, OllamaRerank, OllamaVision
from fitz_ai.llm.instrumentation.token_tracker import wrap_with_token_tracking


# Wrap base Ollama chat provider with token tracking
ollama_chat = OllamaChat()
wrapped_ollama_chat = wrap_with_token_tracking(ollama_chat)
artifacts.purpose: Provider-specific wrapper module for Ollama that wraps the base OllamaChat provider with token tracking functionality.

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify existing system behavior and confirm compatibility with new logic to ensure correctness before writing any code.
deliverables: Confirmed InstrumentedProxy does not currently expose SDK responses in hooks
Verified all chat providers implement ChatProvider or StreamingChatProvider protocols returning str/Iterator[str] types instead of raw usage data objects
key_risks: InstrumentedProxy modification may break existing hooks that don't expect new return types
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import InstrumentedProxy, BenchmarkHook; class MockHook(BenchmarkHook): def __init__(self): self.last_response = None; def on_call_start(self, *args, **kwargs): pass; def on_call_end(self, method_name: str, result=None, duration_sec: float = 0.0): self.last_response = result; hook = MockHook(); proxy = InstrumentedProxy(object(), 'test_layer', 'plugin'); with proxy as p: original_result = {'choices': [{'message': {'content': 'hello'}}]}; setattr(p, '_wrapped_method__', lambda: original_result); result = p._wrapped_method__(); assert hook.last_response is not None and isinstance(hook.last_response, dict); print('✓ InstrumentedProxy can now pass raw response')"

phases.name: Create Minimal TokenTrackingHook for Integration
phases.objective: Implement a minimal tracking solution that logs token usage in existing hooks or via logging module, without requiring major changes to core instrumentation.
deliverables: Created fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_logger.py with TokenUsage dataclass and extract_tokens_from_response function
key_risks: Token extraction fails due to SDK API variability or missing fields in provider responses
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.instrumentation.token_logger import log_token_usage, TokenUsage; import json; response = {'usage': {'prompt_tokens': 100, 'completion_tokens': 200, 'total_tokens': 300}}; log_token_usage('openai', 'chat', response); print('✓ Can log token usage for OpenAI style responses')"

phases.name: Modify Provider Wrappers to Log During Calls
phases.objective: Update provider wrappers (OpenAIChat, AnthropicChat) to call the logger after successful chat calls.
deliverables: Modified fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py to add token logging in chat() method
key_risks: Thread safety of logging during concurrent calls may be broken if not properly synchronized
phases.verification_command: python -c "import fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai as oai; chat = oai.OpenAIChat(); print('✓ OpenAI chat provider initialized')"

phases.name: Test Integration With Existing Systems
phases.objective: Ensure that modified providers now emit logs during query execution without breaking existing functionality.
deliverables: Integration test passes confirming no regression in provider behavior or logging output
key_risks: Silent failure modes where empty responses result in missing token counts instead of defaults

phases.name: Final Validation (End-to-end Functional)
phases.objective: Confirm end-to-end flow works and logs token usage properly during actual queries.
deliverables: CLI or API endpoint triggers a query that goes through tracked provider, logging correct token counts
key_risks: No new security attack surface introduced since feature only adds logging/metric collection capabilities via existing instrumentation infrastructure
phases.verification_command: python -c "import fitz_ai.llm.providers.openai as oai; chat = oai.OpenAIChat(); print('✓ OpenAI chat provider initialized')"

risks.description: InstrumentedProxy modification will break existing hooks that don't expect new return types from wrapped methods
risks.mitigation: Create backward-compatible wrapper that stores raw data in thread-local storage instead of modifying core proxy behavior
risks.contingency: Revert InstrumentedProxy changes and use alternative approach to capture SDK responses for token extraction
risks.verification: Run existing instrumentation tests to ensure no regressions

risks.description: Token extraction fails due to SDK API variability or missing fields in response objects from different LLM providers
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific parsing logic with fallback mechanisms and comprehensive unit testing for each supported SDK format
risks.contingency: Log zero token usage as default when extraction fails silently, ensuring no critical functionality is lost
risks.verification: Write unit tests that mock different provider responses to verify correct parsing behavior

risks.description: Thread safety issues with shared hook storage during concurrent LLM calls may cause incorrect token reporting or data corruption
risks.mitigation: Use thread-local storage to isolate token tracking per call and implement proper synchronization where needed
risks.contingency: Add explicit locking around shared state access in hooks during concurrent execution scenarios
risks.verification: Run concurrent threaded tests with multiple calls to tracked chat providers verifying correct token reporting

risks.description: Streaming response parsing does not aggregate tokens correctly across chunks without invasive changes to streaming logic
risks.mitigation: Modify StreamingChatProvider implementations to capture intermediate token counts or log final totals only after stream completion
risks.contingency: Log total token usage at end of stream instead of attempting real-time aggregation which may require deeper integration changes
risks.verification: Test streaming chat method calls and validate that last_usage.total_tokens reflects the complete session

risks.description: SDK version incompatibilities with token tracking, especially Ollama where usage tracking isn't always supported without raw HTTP parsing
risks.mitigation: Implement robust error handling that gracefully handles missing or malformed token data fields from any provider
risks.contingency: Add fallback to default zero-token logging when SDK-specific usage metadata cannot be parsed reliably
risks.verification: Run integration test with mocked Ollama server returning known structures and verify no crashes occur

risks.description: Token counting skips empty responses or malformed content resulting in silent failures instead of logging zero tokens
risks.mitigation: Implement defensive programming practices including null checks and default values for token fields
risks.contingency: Add explicit fallback to TokenUsage with all zeros when extraction logic encounters missing or invalid data in response objects
risks.verification: Mock provider returning empty dict responses and verify that token logger produces valid zero-count logs instead of exceptions

recommended_contingencies: Revert InstrumentedProxy changes if they break existing instrumentation functionality
Implement fallback logging to zero tokens when SDK-specific parsing fails silently
Use thread-local storage for hook state isolation during concurrent execution scenarios
Add comprehensive error handling around token extraction with graceful degradation
